course_title: Nuts and Bolts of New Ventures
deliverable_target: a faithful, source-conscious lecture-by-lecture chapter sequence for Nuts and Bolts of New Ventures, plus a compiled book that reads like a practical entrepreneurship course rather than a startup hype reel.
visual_target: selected lecture frames are supporting evidence for slide text, diagrams, speaker identity, whiteboard structure, and in-room course context; do not let them override the spoken content. Any newly drawn flowchart, table, or diagram should remain pocket-safe: wrap long labels, prefer taller narrower structure, and keep the result inside \linewidth.
mathematical_standard: preserve concrete claims, business details, legal distinctions, negotiation logic, and any quantitative reasoning from the course, but avoid invented finance or startup mythology that is not in the source.
style_target: notes should read like a careful companion text for venture formation, customer discovery, business-model design, legal structure, team formation, presentation, negotiation, projections, founder motivation, and startup failure modes, with explicit separation between anecdote, mechanism, and recommendation.
credit_target: keep explicit credit to Joseph Hadzima, MIT OpenCourseWare, and curation by LazyingArt LLC; reserve the website URL for front matter, not body prose.
front_matter_single: Original lecture by Joseph Hadzima for MIT OpenCourseWare. Lecture notes organized by \href{https://lazying.art}{LazyingArt LLC}.
front_matter_plural: Original lectures by Joseph Hadzima for MIT OpenCourseWare. Lecture notes organized by \href{https://lazying.art}{LazyingArt LLC}.
reference_missing_text: No directly matching reference PDF is available for this course.
reference_no_match_text: Matching course reference PDFs were found, but no strong topical excerpt was automatically matched for this lecture.
